quote_text: Give thyself leisure to learn some good thing, and cease roving and wandering to and fro.
reflection: Where can you stop scattering your attention today?

quote_text: Of things some are in our power, and others are not.
reflection: What will you place back inside your control this morning?

quote_text: Fire tries gold, misfortune tries brave men.
reflection: What difficulty can you treat as training instead of interruption?

quote_text: As several members in one body united, so are reasonable creatures in a body divided and dispersed.
reflection: Who needs you to act like part of the same body today?

quote_text: If you desire anything which is not in our power, you must be unfortunate.
reflection: What desire would leave you dependent on someone else's choice?

quote_text: Let the mind find out what is good for the mind.
attribution: Seneca, On the Happy Life II
reflection: What good are you pursuing because it is truly good, not merely admired?

quote_text: Good men labour, spend and are spent, and that too of their own free will.
reflection: What worthy effort deserves your willing strength today?

quote_text: The only thing that I must intend, is, that it be good and expedient for the public.
reflection: How can your work serve more than your private preference?

quote_text: Begin then from little things.
reflection: What small act of discipline can you practice before the day grows loud?

quote_text: There is nothing that is new. All things that are, are both usual and of little continuance.
reflection: What problem looks smaller when you remember it has appeared before?

quote_text: Disease is an impediment to the body, but not to the will, unless the will itself chooses.
reflection: Where can your will remain free even if circumstances are constrained?

quote_text: Love is born where hatred abounded.
reflection: Where could you answer division with a more generous first move?

quote_text: Remember that in life you ought to behave as at a banquet.
reflection: What can you receive today without grasping for more?

quote_text: This world is mere change, and this life, opinion.
reflection: Which opinion is making a changing thing feel permanent?

quote_text: Bear them bravely.
reflection: What hard thing asks for steadiness rather than complaint?

quote_text: Reasonable creatures, they are ordained one for another.
reflection: What obligation to another person should guide your next choice?

quote_text: Stretch out your hand and take a portion with decency.
reflection: Where would enough be more dignified than excess today?

quote_text: True wisdom consists in not departing from nature and in moulding our conduct according to her laws and model.
attribution: Seneca, On the Happy Life III
reflection: What would be simpler if you acted with nature instead of against it?

quote_text: That over all might reign one Reason, supreme and eternal.
reflection: How can you honor freedom today by acting from shared reason and justice?

quote_text: If labor (pain) be presented to you, you will find that it is endurance.
reflection: What work can you meet as a chance to build endurance?

quote_text: Do not send your desire forward to it, but wait till it is opposite to you.
reflection: What are you reaching for before its proper time?

quote_text: Look within; within is the fountain of all good.
reflection: What answer are you seeking outside that first needs an inward check?

quote_text: What we want is straightforwardness, and a kindly interpretation of things.
reflection: Where can you choose a fair reading before judging someone?

quote_text: Let not things future trouble thee.
reflection: What future fear can you meet only when it actually arrives?

quote_text: The greatest remedy for anger is delay.
reflection: Where should you wait before answering today?

quote_text: The things in our power are by nature free, not subject to restraint or hindrance.
reflection: What freedom is available before anything outside changes?

quote_text: Finally, love mankind; obey God.
reflection: How can love for people shape one concrete decision today?

quote_text: You can be invincible, if you enter into no contest in which it is not in your power to conquer.
reflection: Which contest should you decline because it cannot improve your character?

quote_text: Nothing, therefore, nourishes anger more than excessive and dissatisfied luxury.
reflection: What comfort has made you less patient than you want to be?

quote_text: It is your good fortune not to need any good fortune.
reflection: What can you release so that luck has less leverage over you?
